metadata.title:
UNION LABOR To
CHOOSE RATE


COMMITTEE

pages.raw_text:
Spokane can secure terminal
rates in return for a franchise to
the North Coast and the Milwaukee
railroads to enter the city. The
railroads can afford to grant Spo-
kane lower rates and not only will
the city of Spokane be benefited,
but the whole Inland Empire will
enjoy the benefits of reduced rates
that can be secured in return for a
franchise" These are arguments
that are today put up by the Peo-
pies Terminal Rate Committee in
a letter to the public. Instances
are recited to substantiate these
assertions.


The letter endorses the city coun-
ellis action in no uncertain Ian
guase for their action in the fran
chise matter and the Peoples Ter
minal Rate committee is now a reg
ularly organized body with head
quarters in the offices of J. B. Val
permanent chairman and Valentine
entine, 40S Empire State building,


B. l.. Gordon has been elected
was chosen permanent secretary at
a meeting of the committee at noon.
The office of treasurer is still I'M
filled, it having oeen decided to
given organized labor an opportun-
ity to place man in that place.
